free_text: Neutron induced fission of 240,242Pu from 1 eV to
200 MeV
LANSCE facility at Los Alamos
Two parallel plate ionization chambers (PPIC)
were used to measure fission fragments.
Time of flight was used to measure incident
neutron energy. Energy given in data section is the
incident neutron energy at the bin center
Cross section taken from
ENDF/B-VII
A proton beam from the LANSCE linear
accelerator is injected into the proton storage ring
PSR with a repetition rate of 20 Hz and a
pulse width of 775 mus. The PSR compresses the pulse
to 125 ns FWHM and the extracted beam is steered to
the Lujan target system, which consists of a pair of
tungsten spallation targets. The spallation
neutrons are moderated by water to shift the neutron
spectrum to lower energies.The WNR 4FP90L flight path
was also used, with the detectors located 10 m from
the bare spallation target
Frame-overlap, off-time beam current  and dead time
corrections were made. The frame-overlap occurs when
fast neutrons from one pulse overtake a slow neutron
produced by a previous pulse, which results in TOF
ambiguity. The off-time beam is a small amount of
current between nominal pulses.
Six different samples were used for this experiment:
239Pu, 240Pu, 241Pu, 242Pu, 235U, and 238U. The 239Pu
and 241Pu  samples were used for background
subtraction, since these isotopes were present as
contaminants in the 240Pu and 242Pu samples. The 238U
sample was used to monitor accelerator off-time
current. Cross section were measured as a ratio to
235U(n,f) cross section.
Comment by author Fredrik Tovesson (2020-10-10):
Generation of ratios and extraction of statistical
uncertainties is impossible anymore
Uncertainty in over all normalisation,
not included in DATA-ERR
Combined statistical uncertainty and energy
dependent systematic uncertainties
Data sent by author (F.T.)
Waiting for data in resonance region 1-1000 eV.
